mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, which systematically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 over a five-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates creates a favorable liquidity and demand environment that amplifies equity risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression, or signal loses predictive power when either series is stationary.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening real economic activity and corporate output capacity, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained inflationary periods, yielding negative returns when INDPRO rises greater than 1 percent quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly drives expanding corporate profit pools, establishing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Three-year rolling correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.15, or returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of high-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, it reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, indicating market adaptation to high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion and consumer confidence, which jointly drive institutional capital allocation into equities and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when housing starts are driven by speculative bubbles rather than fundamental demand, evidenced by negative returns despite rising PAYEMS and HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates a broad economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of periods where both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or the combined signal shows no statistical edge over single-series baselines.
